response.justification: The candidate has a broad software engineering background with genuine strengths in high-throughput distributed systems and software craftsmanship, but falls critically short on the role's single most important hard requirement: 8 years of C++ experience. The resume shows only roughly 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project — nowhere near the mandatory threshold. The primary stack throughout the career is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is also no evidence of technical leadership on a cloud compute platform or C++-based infrastructure at scale, making this a poor fit for both the minimum and preferred qualifications.
strengths: Hands-on experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, low-latency design)
Strong software craftsmanship culture including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active knowledge-sharing via workshops and conference talks
Broad infrastructure exposure spanning Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker, and microservices — some relevance to cloud platform work
concerns: Virtually no C++ experience — the role mandates 8 years, but the resume shows only ~6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a student thesis project
No evidence of owning or leading a cloud compute platform; cloud work is limited to AWS CDK usage within a product team, not platform engineering
Core language expertise (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) is misaligned with the C++-centric infrastructure and systems focus of the role
